strong.memo: Do not authorise the full confirmatory budget until the data-definition and cash-quality gates clear. Reported ARR includes EUR 1.30m not live or invoiced, while the bridge implies 105.8% NRR rather than management's 121% [D02][D08]. Expansion quality is concentrated in CPI and one acquired-site roll-in, and Customer A both represents 12.4% of ARR and retenders in nine months [D03][D07]. Reported EBITDA also depends on EUR 2.10m of capitalised development and EUR 1.40m of excluded onboarding payroll; cash from operations does not cover capitalised development [D06]. The product may still own a useful regulated workflow, but the DACH proof is reseller-led and implementation is slower and more service-heavy than presented [D05][D08][D09]. Run A01, A02, A03 and A04 first. Proceed only if deployed and invoiced ARR reconciles, Customer A renewal risk is containable, and normalised cash economics support the valuation discussion.

thesis_breakers.title: Cash earnings are materially below adjusted EBITDA

conditions: Reconcile contracted, deployed and invoiced ARR
Restate EBITDA after capitalised development and excluded payroll
strong.dissenting_interpretation: A buyer could accept temporary delivery intensity if the customer export proves high retention after go-live and the workflow has genuine switching costs.

weak.memo: Norwyn is an attractive European software leader with 121% NRR, 31% growth, 87% gross margins and a large EUR 8.5bn TAM. The company has strong DACH momentum and an AI-ready product. The main risks are competition, regulation and macroeconomic uncertainty. Management should keep investing in sales and product while reducing services. The 7.0 times ARR valuation is reasonable for a category leader, so the team should authorise diligence.
weak.dissenting_interpretation: The company may grow more slowly.
